content:
3 min read
Just now

--

Humans used to gain knowledge through years of studying, mentorship, and practice. Today, you can prompt an LLM and it will spit out 1000s of words per minute, most of which you do not understand. You would apply the solutions to your project, feel accomplished for the day, and the story continues the next day.
However, what did you learn from the prompt sessions and outputs? Nothing.
Transferable skills are those hardwired knowledge that you can teach someone even in your subconscious. They are gained through years rigorous training, learning, and hands-on practice. As long as humans are concerned, nothing will replace experiential learning.
When you prompt an Artificial Intelligence system, you suddenly feel like an expert in a field you didn’t invest in. As the addiction to this instant-expertise phenomenon continues, no one will invest in learning any more. And when humans stop learning: researches stop, real innovations stop, and development stagnates and later declines.
Last Friday, I spent the whole day drafting a system design document for a company. It was 100% hand-written with zero input from an LLM. As I was writing, I could literally feel the flow of creativity from my brain as my brain synthesised all my experience into tangible words on the document. An LLM without access to my unique experiences in life or my innermost thoughts wouldn’t have been able to draft such a document in such a personal way. Whatever an LLM could have helped me draft would have felt very distant and synthetic.
The only cost was time. While an LLM could have spat out the document in 5 minutes after some thorough prompts, I spent the entire day drafting the document. However, that time cost is the cost of creativity. And creativity is what we are willing sacrificing on the altar of speed and instant gratification.
Today, many have already become literal hostages to LLMs. They cannot do anything without prompting an LLM. They no longer believe they have it in them. Or can learn something new on their own and attempt to apply the knowledge without an LLM. Once their daily limit is exhausted, they feel completely helpless and go into downtime instantly.
The trajectory descends into despair for humanity. Of all technologies humans have created, LLMs and artificial intelligence seems to be the one which will cause a gross deceleration in human intelligence and proficiency. The touted increase in productivity from the use of LLMs is a trojan horse. The ultimate objectives are the capture and destruction of human intelligence, the complete and irreversible dependency of humans on artificial intelligence, and the sale and metering of intelligence by corporations.
This is an early warning. You can call it a prophecy — like the prophecy of Jonah to the people of Nineveh. Some prophecies are sent out so that humans can change course like the people of Nineveh did.
So what can you do?

• Reduce your dependency on LLM. Don’t destroy your creativity and intelligence and have them sold back to you by companies.

• Don’t stop learning new things from scratch the hard way because of instant gratification from LLMs.

• If you run an educational institution or training programme, double down on the ban of LLMs by students. Let students learn the hard, painful way which is true learning. Humanity depends on it.
Finally, this was written with zero LLM input and review. To many, it is already an impossible task.
Redemption starts now.
